tags.name: Carrier
tags.description:
The Carrier is the entity that allows how to deliver a Shipment. Is the connector between the Shipping Type and the Company.

It has customizable properties as follows:

- It should have a Shipping Type and a `shippingTypeId` field related to that Shipping Type.
- It could have a Company `companyId` with his Integration `integrationId`.
- It could have an Ecommerce platform and an `ecommerceAccountId` related to that Ecommerce Account.
- If the Shipment Type allow Shipping Windows then the Carrier could have `shippingWindows`.

tags.name: Carrier Group
tags.description:
The `Carrier Group` entity is used to group together a set of carriers that share the same time slot.

- The carrier group can be grouped by locations or carriers.
- The group's carriers share the same delivery quota.
- The carrier group determines which carriers' time slots to block if the set limit is reached.

**IMPORTANT**
- If we configure the group with locations, the carriers that are linked to those locations will be grouped together.
- If we configure the group with carriers, the configured carriers will be grouped.

tags.name: Carrier Group Time Slot
tags.description:
This entity specifies the delivery schedules and capacity limits for carriers within a group. It defines the available time ranges and maximum shipping capacity for grouped carriers.

**IMPORTANT**
- If the time slot is locked, all associated carrier time slots that match will also be locked.
- If the time slot is unlocked, all associated carrier time slots that match will also be unlocked.

tags.name: Company
tags.description:
The company service is in charge of managing the properties that define the flow of a shipment as if it uses
an external logistics service, the integration credentials or complements, the package generation, among others.

Currently the service has the following integrations (external logistics services):

- DHL
- Ivoy
- Exxe

> If the company has integration, the types of labels available and packages usage will be determined by it, and the
> company can receive credentials and integration complements

> If the company has set the flag use packages, this affects the shipment, making it be scheduled only until it has
> packages associated

> We have the following label formats if company does not have integration:
> - PDF

tags.name: Delivery Area Availability
tags.description:
The delivery area availability is an entity that allows for the configuration of available and unavailable times for deliveries,
with the aim of preventing the simulation from returning delivery windows that would prevent the driver from accessing the delivery area.

These are the simulation APIs where delivery area availability is used:

- **Delivery**:
  - [API Simulate](https://docs.janisdev.in/v2/service/delivery#operation/makeSimulation)
- **Delivery Public**:
  - [API Simulate](https://docs.janisdev.in/v2/service/delivery-public#operation/bySimulation)
  - [API Simulate By SLA](https://docs.janisdev.in/v2/service/delivery-public#operation/bySlaSimulation)

tags.description:
To handle system integration and their respective properties and functionalities.

The functionalities that define an integration are:

  - complements

  - useLabel

> This entity is mainly used by:

tags.name: Time Slot Export
tags.description:
Time slot export functionality provides CSV exports of slot availability data for external analysis and reporting. Exports are generated automatically every 15 minutes when changes are detected and made available through administrative APIs.

Key features:
- Automated generation every 15 minutes
- CSV format with semicolon separators
- 30-day retention policy
- Administrative visibility and download URLs

tags.name: Time Slot Feed
tags.description:
Time slot feed APIs provide external systems with access to pending export files for automated processing. The feed includes temporary download URLs and supports batch status updates for processed files.

Key features:
- Temporary download URLs (1 hour validity)
- Batch processing confirmation
- Designed for automated client integration

post.description:
This API will return a list of available delivery options for a given shipping SLA.
This delivery options can be narrowed by sending an origin Location ID, the SKUs to send and a dropoff location (required for delivery SLAs).

The following validations will be run for each delivery options:

1. The shipping SLA must exist and must be enabled
2. There must be active carriers with the SLA and a coverage area that includes the dropoff (if the SLA is for store pickup and dropoff is not sent, coverage area check will be skipped)
3. Carriers must be related to at least one Location. And this Locations must have active Warehouses with the proper tasks enabled
4. Carriers without windows must have Bussiness days set if the [Validate express carrier business days](https://app.janis.in/delivery/setting/edit) setting is enabled
5. Carriers with windows must have a window schema that generates windows that fit the carrier's minimum and maximum delivery time
6. If SKUs are sent, carrier restrictions (total volume, total weight and per item dimensions) will be enforced
7. If SKUs are sent, they must all have stock in at least a single Warehouse (among the active Warehouses related to the carrier)

**Info**
- Carriers with windows where time slots exist but they have been locked will be considered as available, but each time slot will include information about the lock status and reason.
- To see the locked time slots, the user must have the `delivery:simulate:show-blocked-slots` permission.

If debug mode (see `debug` field in request body) is active, unavailable carriers will be returned with a `unavailabilityMessage` property that indicates which of the rules above was met.
If a carrier is not returned in debug mode, it means that it has a different SLA, it's inactive or its coverage area does not include the dropoff.

If debug mode is not active, only available carriers will be returned. **Debug mode** is not recommended for production environments, it is intended for development and testing purposes only.

put.description:
Updates the shipping creation cutoff times configuration for a company.
This endpoint allows setting time windows with specific days and cutoff times
that determine when shippings can be created for the company.

**Requirements:**
- Company must have an integration configured
- Time windows cannot have duplicate times
- Days within a window cannot be duplicated
- If timezone is not provided, the client's timezone will be used

When a holiday fails to be processed or is reverted, this API can be used to reapply the holiday.

This can only be executed for holidays in the `error` or `reverted` status.

Before executing this for failed holidays, it's recommended to check the `timeSlots[].error` property of the holiday to check for error messages.

When a holiday is already processed, this API can revert it's changes unlocking time slots that are currently available.

This can only be executed for holidays in the `error` or `done` status.

This API generates a delivery note for the route

This process is available for those routes that have a  [Company](/v2/service/delivery#tag/Company) related to the `useDeliveryNote` property set.

With the information of the route and its related entities it will try to generate a [Document](/v2/service/document-generator#tag/Document)
through the Document Generator Service, using the [Template](/v2/service/document-generator#tag/TemplateDocument) with the `route-delivery-note` template code.

In case of being able to generate it successfully in the route, the identifier of this record will be saved in the property 'deliveryNoteId'.

post.summary: Notify that shipping is ready for operator creation
post.description:
Notifies that a shipping is ready to be created in the logistics operator system. This endpoint validates that:

- The shipping exists
- The shipping has integration defined
- The shipping is in `created` status
- The shipping has the `pendingOperatorNotification` flag set to `true`

If all validations pass, it will:
- Set `pendingOperatorNotification` to `false`
- Emit the `shippingReadyForOperatorCreation` topic to notify that the shipping is ready for creation in the logistics operator

The update is performed with a safety filter to ensure no concurrent processing.
If SNS publication fails, the update is automatically rolled back.

parameters.description: This refer to the `id` of the [Shipping](/v2/service/tms#tag/Shipping)

  .  Delivery type equal to : `delivery` or `express_delivery`.

  .  Shipping without associated route

Indicate whether to apply the overlap in the `scheduleRange` filter.

**IMPORTANT**: Should send `scheduleRange` filter to apply this filter.

- If `true`, the `scheduleRange` filter will be applied to the shippings whose scheduled time range (start–end) overlaps with the specified range.
- If `false`, the `scheduleRange` filter will be applied to the shippings whose scheduled within the range specified.

displayId.description:
It is a unique reference ID that is generated and assigned automatically when the record is created, it cannot be modified and it is structured in three parts:

- **Date**: It is the start date of the wave in the format `YYMMDD`.
- **Delimiter**: It is the character `-`.

status.description:
The shipping container status.

- **pending**: It is the initial and default status of shipping container.
- **approved**: It is the status to indicate that the shipping container has been consolidated.
- **scheduled**: It is the status to indicate that the shipping container is scheduled to be dispatched by the logistics operator.
- **dispatched**: It is the status to indicate that the shipping container has been delivered by the logistics operator.
- **delivered**: It is the status to indicate that all packages in the shipping container have been delivered.
- **notDelivered**: It is the status to indicate that all packages in the shipping container have not been delivered.
- **partiallyDelivered**: It is the status to indicate that some of the packages in the shipping container have not been delivered.
- **error**: It is the status to indicate that an error occurred when trying to communicate with the logistics operator integration.

discrepancy.description:
Discrepancy between the delivery cost paid by the customer and the amount reported by the logistics operator.
This field will only be generated when a delivery cost is present.
If the customer's currency differs from the operator's currency, the discrepancy will be calculated based on the operator's currency, as well as in United States Dollars (amountInUSD).

If the discrepancy is positive, it means that the customer paid more than the delivery cost reported by the logistics operator.
Otherwise, it indicates that the delivery cost reported by the logistics operator was higher than what the customer paid.
